security_audit.summary: This skill has low-risk features: external_commands. No dangerous patterns detected.

content.user_title: Automate Django backend code quality and atomic commits
content.value_statement: This skill enforces strict engineering standards for Django/Optimo-style repositories by automating pre-commit checks and fixes. It actively corrects code formatting, imports, type hints, and logging to match local rules, pre-commit configurations, and security helpers. By ensuring commits are atomic and generating clean, ticket-prefixed messages without AI signatures, it significantly reduces review friction and maintains high code hygiene. It integrates deeply with tools like Ruff and Django checks to catch errors early, ensuring that every commit is safe, reviewable, and compliant with the team's backend taste.
actual_capabilities: Automatically fix code formatting, imports, and type hints
Enforce structured logging and type safety (TypedDict)
Run pre-commit hooks and security scripts (.security/*)
Generate ticket-prefixed commit messages without AI signatures
Integrate with local repo rules (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md)
limitations: Optimized specifically for Django/Optimo-style backend repositories
Requires specific tooling (uv, ruff, ty) to be present or configured
Falls back to generic Python behavior if manage.py or backend markers are missing

use_cases.title: Pre-commit Code Cleanup
use_cases.description: Automatically fix formatting, imports, and type hints for all modified files to comply with backend standards before committing.

use_cases.title: Atomic Commit Preparation
use_cases.description: Ensure staged changes are atomic, pass all quality gates (Ruff, Django checks), and generate a compliant commit message.

prompt_templates.title: Fix and Format Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Run `/backend-atomic-commit:pre-commit` on this repo and actively fix all files in `git status` so they obey backend `AGENTS.md`, `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, `.security/*` helpers, and Monty’s taste.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Atomic Commit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use `/backend-atomic-commit:atomic-commit` to prepare an atomic commit for the staged changes in `backend/`. Enforce all pre-commit hooks and `.security` scripts, then propose a ticket-prefixed commit message.

output: Proposed commit message: [TICKET-123] Fix user login bug

best_practices: Use this skill before every commit to ensure code quality and consistency.
Ensure AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md reflect current team standards for the skill to follow.
Keep commits atomic and focused on a single logical change to pass atomicity checks.
anti_patterns: Ignoring [BLOCKING] issues reported by the skill.
Using the skill for frontend or non-Python repositories expecting full functionality.

faq.question: Does this skill add AI signatures to commit messages?
faq.answer: No, it explicitly ensures commit messages look human-written and contain no AI signatures or plugin branding.

faq.question: What happens if I am not in a Django repo?
faq.answer: The skill detects the environment and falls back to a lighter 'generic Python pre-commit' behavior.
